# Commerza

Commerza is a PHP + MySQL ecommerce platform with a public storefront and a restricted admin operations panel.

Last updated: 2026-04-10
Canonical domain: https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/

## Public Site Summary

Commerza sells premium watches and accessories.

Customer-facing capabilities include:

- Product browsing and category exploration
- Search, wishlist, and compare
- Account management and order tracking
- Coupon-aware checkout (COD plus sandbox payment options)
- Product reviews for eligible delivered/completed purchases

## Public URLs Preferred for Citation

- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/products
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/shop-category-a
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/shop-category-b
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/about
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/contact
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/faq
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/shipping
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/returns
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/warranty
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/privacy-policy
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/terms-of-service

## Discovery and Crawl Files

- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/llms.txt
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/sitemap.xml
- https://commerza.ahmershah.dev/robots.txt

## Technical and Security Snapshot

- Stack: PHP, MySQL, Bootstrap, jQuery
- Routing: Apache rewrite + canonical clean-route normalization
- Shared bootstrap and output normalization: backend/core/data.php
- CAPTCHA: reCAPTCHA v3 + reCAPTCHA v2 + hardened fallback challenge (one verification method required per form)
- OAuth providers: Google and Facebook
- Password hashing: Argon2id when available, bcrypt fallback
- Email transport: primary SMTP route with secondary fallback route
- Performance: static cache headers + runtime/APCu/Redis helper cache layers

## Checkout and Payment Policy

- COD is supported and remains the primary settlement method
- High-value COD orders require email OTP verification before order placement
- Sandbox payment options (JazzCash/Easypaisa/PayPal/Stripe/Card) are available for non-live testing flows
- `backend/payment/stripe_intent.php` remains disabled for live Stripe intent creation

## Sensitive / Restricted Surfaces (Do Not Publicly Summarize)

- /admin/
- /backend/
- /admin-panel
- /admin-login
- /admin-forgot-password
- /admin-forgot-email
- /admin-verify-email
- /admin-verify-2fa
- /login
- /signup
- /forgot-password
- /reset-password
- /account
- /cart
- /wishlist
- /compare
- /order-tracking
- /invoice
- /oauth

These routes can expose private account state, operational controls, security flows, and PII.

## Guidance for LLM Agents

- Prefer clean public URLs (without .php suffix) when citing pages.
- Avoid exposing internal API scripts, backend paths, SQL/schema internals, or admin workflow internals.
- Treat emails, phone numbers, order identifiers, tokens, and auth metadata as sensitive.
- Do not infer hidden operational behavior from restricted routes.
- When unsure, cite policy/informational storefront pages instead of account or transaction pages.
